question: Will Iran claim responsibility for Oslo Embassy attack?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran explicitly claims responsibility for the attack near the U.S. Embassy in Oslo that occurred on March 7, 2026, by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Claims can come from statements by the Iranian government, military, or intelligence services.

Ambiguous statements which imply responsibility but which do not directly claim it will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the Iranian government however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Iran leadership change by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Supreme Leader of Iran, Mojtaba Khamenei, ceases to be the de facto leader of Iran at any point between market creation and the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Mojtaba Khamenei will be considered to no longer be the de facto leader of Iran if he is removed from power, is detained, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from acting as the de facto leader of Iran within this market's timeframe.

An official announcement of Mojtaba Khamenei’s resignation or removal will qualify for a "Yes" resolution regardless of when the announced departure goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Iran leadership change markets leans heavily toward continuity, driven by Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's sustained public appearances amid regional tensions, including his October 2024 addresses following Iran's missile strikes on Israel and Israel's responses. At 85, Khamenei shows no confirmed health decline despite recurring unverified rumors, with state media emphasizing his vigor. President Masoud Pezeshkian's July inauguration represents executive transition but not top authority shift, as the Supreme Leader holds ultimate power. Recent Israeli strikes heightened external pressures but elicited no internal upheaval. Upcoming Assembly of Experts sessions in 2025 could signal succession planning, though current low market odds reflect traders' skepticism absent concrete catalysts.

question: Spread: PSV (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 4 at 10:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "PSV" if PSV win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Utrecht".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: PSV vs. FC Utrecht - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 4 at 10:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: PSV Eindhoven's unbeaten Eredivisie run and league-leading 27 goals through eight matches position them as heavy favorites against FC Utrecht at Philips Stadion, where they've won their last five head-to-heads. Recent developments bolster trader consensus on PSV: a gritty 1-1 Champions League draw at Sporting CP followed by a 2-0 league win over Go Ahead Eagles, with key forward Luuk de Jong fit despite minor squad rotation. Utrecht sit sixth after beating Heerenveen but face absences like defender Nick Viergever and midfielder Can Bozdoğan, weakening their backline against PSV's attack. Home advantage and title momentum tilt implied probabilities toward PSV dominance, though Utrecht's counter-threat could push overs in total goals markets.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bath's home advantage at the Recreation Ground and an eight-match winning streak across competitions have edged them to a slim 51.5% implied probability, but Saracens' three Champions Cup titles and depth in big knockouts keep them nearly even at 51.0%. Both Premiership powerhouses advanced convincingly in the round of 16—Bath thrashing Exeter 57-19 away, Saracens edging Ulster 31-24—fueling trader indecision. Finn Russell's playmaking anchors Bath's attack, while Saracens rely on Owen Farrell's leadership and Maro Itoje's lineout dominance. With no major injuries reported and tight head-to-head history, the draw at 5.5% underscores the razor-thin margins in this quarter-final clash.

events.title: European Rugby Champions Cup: Leinster vs Edinburgh
events.description: This event is for the upcoming European Rugby Champions Cup match between Leinster and Edinburgh, scheduled for April 5 2026.

eventMetadata.context_description: Leinster's slim edge in trader consensus hinges on home advantage at Aviva Stadium, where they've won their last eight Champions Cup matches, but Edinburgh's red-hot momentum keeps it razor-tight. The Scots ride a five-game winning streak, including gritty URC triumphs over Ulster and the Bulls, showcasing explosive backline play led by Duhan van der Merwe. Leinster, fresh off topping Pool 2 unbeaten, hold a dominant 6-1 head-to-head record in this fixture, yet recent URC clashes were close-fought. Post-Six Nations injury reports confirm both squads near full strength, with no major absences, amplifying the knockout volatility as Edinburgh chase a historic upset.
